summary: Adrian Beker answered the question positively in arXiv:2309.12809 (2023), constructing an explicit 5-element set in ℝ² that has a protrusive ordering not realised by any finite multiset of vantage points, i.e., not contained in Ψ(C). The paper explicitly states it answers a question of Alon, Defant, Kravitz, and Zhu, and was published in Archiv der Mathematik (DOI 10.1007/s00013-024-02020-x).

since_posted.title: A protrusive ordering of 5 points not witnessed by any finite multiset
since_posted.claim: Constructs an explicit 5-element set C ⊆ ℝ² with a protrusive ordering that cannot be obtained by ranking points by distance-sum to any finite multiset, thereby answering the open question of Alon–Defant–Kravitz–Zhu affirmatively.

search_queries: protrusive ordering vantage points arXiv 2308.05208 Alon Defant Kravitz Zhu
protrusive ordering candidates witnessing set five points plane geometry
cite:2308.05208 protrusive ordering five points Psi witnessed 2024 2025
notes: The question is resolved: a 5-point counterexample exists (yes). Beker's paper arXiv:2309.12809 was submitted in September 2023, only weeks after the source paper, and published in Archiv der Mathematik in 2024.
